message:
fix(ci): use exact version comparison in release version assertion

The grep-based substring check had two bugs:
1. Dots treated as regex wildcards (e.g., "0.1.3" matches "0X1Z3")
2. Substring match allows false positives (e.g., tag v0.1.3 would
   incorrectly pass if stale binary reports 0.1.31)

Extract the version token from clap's "br X.Y.Z" output and compare
with string equality instead. Applied to both Unix and Windows steps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -128,24 +128,28 @@ jobs:
         run: |
           EXPECTED="${{ github.ref_name }}"
           EXPECTED="${EXPECTED#v}"  # strip leading v
-          ACTUAL=$(./target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/br --version 2>&1)
-          if ! echo "$ACTUAL" | grep -q "$EXPECTED"; then
-            echo "ERROR: Binary reports '$ACTUAL' but expected version '$EXPECTED'"
+          RAW=$(./target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/br --version 2>&1)
+          # clap outputs "br X.Y.Z"; extract the version token
+          ACTUAL=$(echo "$RAW" | awk '{print $NF}')
+          if [ "$ACTUAL" != "$EXPECTED" ]; then
+            echo "ERROR: Binary reports version '$ACTUAL' (from '$RAW') but expected '$EXPECTED'"
             exit 1
           fi
-          echo "Version check passed: $ACTUAL contains $EXPECTED"
+          echo "Version check passed: $ACTUAL == $EXPECTED"
 
       - name: Verify binary version matches tag (Windows)
         if: runner.os == 'Windows'
         shell: pwsh
         run: |
           $expected = "${{ github.ref_name }}" -replace '^v', ''
-          $actual = & ./target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/br.exe --version 2>&1 | Out-String
-          if ($actual -notmatch [regex]::Escape($expected)) {
-            Write-Error "ERROR: Binary reports '$actual' but expected version '$expected'"
+          $raw = & ./target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/br.exe --version 2>&1 | Out-String
+          # clap outputs "br X.Y.Z"; extract the version token
+          $actual = ($raw.Trim() -split '\s+')[-1]
+          if ($actual -ne $expected) {
+            Write-Error "ERROR: Binary reports version '$actual' (from '$raw') but expected '$expected'"
             exit 1
           }
-          Write-Host "Version check passed: $actual contains $expected"
+          Write-Host "Version check passed: $actual == $expected"
 
       - name: Create archive (Unix)
         if: runner.os != 'Windows'
